title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb: Authentic Turkish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb with our easy Turkish recipe. Get tender, flavorful lamb perfect for a special dinner. Step-by-step guide included!

seo.h1: Authentic Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb Recipe
h2Headings: What is Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb?
How to Make Kuzu Tandir (Step-by-Step Guide)
Tips for Perfectly Tender Slow Roasted Lamb
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully slow-roasted Kuzu Tandir, tender Turkish lamb dish, garnished and ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Our Kuzu Tandir recipe yields incredibly tender and flavorful Turkish slow-roasted lamb, perfect for a special dinner.

description: Experience the melt-in-your-mouth tenderness of Kuzu Tandir, a classic Turkish slow-roasted lamb dish. This recipe captures the essence of traditional Anatolian cooking, delivering incredibly flavorful lamb that falls off the bone. Perfect for a festive meal or a comforting family dinner, it promises an unforgettable culinary journey.

ingredients.item: Lamb shoulder or leg (bone-in preferred for flavor)

instructions.title: Prepare and Season the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep incisions all over the lamb. Rub the lamb generously with olive oil, salt, black pepper, red pepper flakes (if using), and dried oregano, ensuring the seasoning gets into the incisions.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb for Flavor
instructions.text: Heat a large, oven-safe Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until deeply browned, about 3-5 minutes per side. This crucial step builds immense flavor. Remove the lamb and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Add the chopped onions and smashed garlic to the same pot. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and fragrant, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot to incorporate into the sauce.

instructions.title: Assemble for Slow Roasting
instructions.text: Return the seared lamb to the pot, nestling it among the softened onions and garlic. Add the bay leaves and pour in the hot water or lamb broth. The liquid should come about halfway up the side of the lamb.

instructions.title: Begin the Slow Roast
instructions.text: Bring the liquid to a gentle simmer on the stovetop. Cover the pot tightly with its lid or heavy-duty foil. Transfer to a preheated oven at 150°C (300°F).

instructions.title: Roast Until Tender
instructions.text: Roast for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone with a fork. The exact time may vary based on your oven and the size of the lamb cut.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Carefully remove the pot from the oven. Let the lamb rest, covered, for at least 15-20 minutes before carving or shredding. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness and flavor. Discard the bay leaves and serve hot with its rich pan juices.

tips: For the most tender lamb, ensure your pot is tightly sealed. If using foil, create a very good seal to trap all the moisture and steam, replicating a traditional tandir oven.
Do not skip the searing step! It develops a crucial layer of flavor (Maillard reaction) that will deepen the overall taste and color of your Kuzu Tandir.
Allowing the lamb to rest after cooking is essential. It helps the meat reabsorb its juices, resulting in a more succulent and moist final product when served.
article: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb is a beloved Turkish Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
